services.name: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
services.description: Centralized identity and access control for all AWS services. Supports users, groups, roles, and policies with fine-grained permissions. Enables federation with external identity providers via SAML 2.0 and OIDC. Supports attribute-based access control (ABAC) and service control policies in Organizations.

services.name: AWS Organizations
services.description: Centralized management of multiple AWS accounts with hierarchical organizational units (OUs). Service Control Policies (SCPs) provide guardrails that restrict permissions across member accounts regardless of IAM policies. Essential for multi-account security strategies.

services.name: Amazon GuardDuty
services.description: Managed threat detection service that continuously monitors for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior. Analyzes CloudTrail management and data events, VPC Flow Logs, DNS logs, EKS audit logs, RDS login activity, Lambda network activity, and S3 data plane events using machine learning and threat intelligence.

services.name: AWS Security Hub
services.description: Central security findings aggregator that collects, normalizes, and prioritizes findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, Firewall Manager, IAM Access Analyzer, and third-party tools using AWS Security Finding Format (ASFF). Runs automated compliance checks against CIS AWS Foundations, AWS Foundational Security Best Practices, PCI DSS, and NIST 800-53.

services.name: AWS CloudTrail
services.description: Records all API calls made in an AWS account as events, providing a complete audit trail of actions taken through the console, CLI, SDKs, and other AWS services. Management events are logged by default for 90 days. Trail creation enables long-term storage in S3 with optional CloudWatch Logs integration.

services.name: AWS Config
services.description: Continuously records AWS resource configurations and evaluates them against desired configurations using Config Rules. Provides a configuration timeline showing how resources changed over time. Conformance packs bundle related rules for frameworks like CIS, PCI DSS, and NIST.

services.name: Amazon Macie
services.description: ML-powered data security service that automatically discovers, classifies, and protects sensitive data stored in Amazon S3. Identifies PII, financial data, credentials, and custom data types. Generates findings when sensitive data is found in buckets with public access or lacking encryption.

services.name: AWS Key Management Service (KMS)
services.description: Managed service for creating and controlling cryptographic keys used to encrypt data across AWS services. Supports symmetric and asymmetric keys, automatic key rotation, and key policies for fine-grained access control. Integrated with 100+ AWS services for server-side encryption.

services.name: Amazon Inspector
services.description: Automated vulnerability management service that continually scans EC2 instances, container images in ECR, and Lambda functions for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. Uses the CVE database and network reachability analysis to generate prioritized findings with risk scores.

services.name: AWS WAF
services.description: Web application firewall that protects web applications from common exploits. Deployed on CloudFront, ALB, API Gateway, AppSync, or Cognito. Supports managed rule groups (AWS, marketplace), custom rules with rate limiting, geo-blocking, IP reputation lists, and bot control.

services.name: AWS Shield
services.description: Managed DDoS protection service. Shield Standard provides automatic protection against common L3/L4 attacks for all AWS customers at no cost. Shield Advanced adds L7 protection, real-time visibility, DDoS Response Team access, and cost protection for scaling during attacks.

services.name: Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC)
services.description: Isolated virtual network for AWS resources with full control over IP addressing, subnets, route tables, and gateways. Security groups provide stateful instance-level firewalling. Network ACLs provide stateless subnet-level controls. VPC Flow Logs capture network traffic metadata.

services.name: AWS PrivateLink
services.description: Provides private connectivity between VPCs, AWS services, and on-premises networks without exposing traffic to the public internet. Creates interface VPC endpoints powered by ENIs in your subnets. Eliminates the need for internet gateways, NAT devices, or VPN connections for accessing supported AWS services.

services.name: AWS Secrets Manager
services.description: Securely stores, rotates, and retrieves database credentials, API keys, and other secrets. Supports automatic rotation with built-in Lambda functions for RDS, Redshift, and DocumentDB. Secrets are encrypted with KMS and can be replicated across regions.

services.name: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)
services.description: Provisions, manages, and deploys SSL/TLS certificates for use with AWS services. Public certificates are free and auto-renew. Private CA capability enables issuing private certificates for internal resources. Integrated with ELB, CloudFront, API Gateway, and other services.

services.name: Amazon Detective
services.description: Analyzes and visualizes security data to rapidly investigate potential security issues. Automatically collects log data from CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, GuardDuty findings, and EKS audit logs, then uses ML and graph analysis to build a unified interactive view for root cause analysis.
keyFeatures: Integration with GuardDuty and Security Hub

services.name: IAM Access Analyzer
services.description: Analyzes resource-based policies to identify resources shared with external entities. Validates IAM policies against best practices and generates policies based on access activity. Identifies unused access (roles, access keys, passwords) to support least privilege.

services.name: AWS Firewall Manager
services.description: Central security management service that allows you to configure and manage firewall rules across accounts and resources in AWS Organizations. Manages WAF rules, Shield Advanced protections, VPC security groups, Network Firewall policies, and Route 53 Resolver DNS Firewall rules from a single pane.

services.name: AWS Control Tower
services.description: Service for setting up and governing a secure, multi-account AWS environment based on AWS best practices. Provides a landing zone with pre-configured accounts, mandatory and elective guardrails (preventive SCPs and detective Config rules), and account factory for standardized provisioning.

services.name: AWS Audit Manager
services.description: Continuously audits AWS usage to simplify risk and compliance assessment. Maps AWS activity to controls in frameworks like CIS, PCI DSS, SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA, and custom frameworks. Automates evidence collection from Config, CloudTrail, Security Hub, and manual uploads.
keyFeatures: Integration with Config and Security Hub

services.name: AWS Network Firewall
services.description: Managed network firewall service for VPCs providing stateful inspection, intrusion prevention (IPS), and web filtering. Supports Suricata-compatible IPS rules, domain filtering, and custom rule groups. Deployed in firewall subnets with traffic routing via VPC route tables.

services.name: AWS Verified Access
services.description: Provides secure access to corporate applications without requiring a VPN. Validates each access request in real-time against identity and device security policies using trust providers (IAM Identity Center, Okta, CrowdStrike, Jamf). Implements zero-trust network access principles.

services.name: AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO)
services.description: Centrally manages workforce access to multiple AWS accounts and business applications. Provides single sign-on with built-in identity store or federation with external IdPs (Active Directory, Okta, Azure AD). Permission sets define access levels for AWS accounts.

services.name: Amazon CloudWatch
services.description: Monitoring and observability service that collects metrics, logs, and events from AWS resources and applications. CloudWatch Logs stores and queries log data. CloudWatch Alarms trigger notifications and automated actions. Contributor Insights identifies top contributors to operational issues.
